metadata.title: Fetch Google Calendar Events and Store Locally

story.iWant: to fetch my Google Calendar events and store them locally
story.soThat: I can view my calendar offline and keep a local cache of my existing events
story.tasks: - Reuse Story 2.1 authentication and settings surface - Extend GoogleCalendarService with paginated full-sync fetch methods - Add GcalEventDto, SyncProgress, SyncResult, and SyncManager - Add SyncToken persistence to DataSourceRefresh via EF migration - Upsert fetched events into gcal_event using the existing entity - Add manual sync UI, progress display, and cancellation - Add unit and integration tests for pagination, mapping, persistence, and cancellation

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Manual sync fetches all events for the default 6-month-back / 1-month-forward range after authentication.
Pagination is exhaustive; all pages are fetched before sync completes.
Progress is visible and cancellation stops further API fetches cleanly while preserving already-written rows.
Timed, all-day, deleted, and recurring-instance events are mapped correctly into the existing GcalEvent schema using UTC-normalized timestamps.
Successful sync writes or updates gcal_event rows, audit log metadata, data_source_refresh metadata, and the returned nextSyncToken.
Empty calendars complete successfully without user-facing errors.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines the canonical full-sync flow: fetch paginated events, upsert into gcal_event, mark deleted rows, update data_source_refresh, and log sync completion.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Story 2.2 requires configured-range fetch, exhaustive pagination, progress plus cancellation, correct all-day and recurring-event mapping, and graceful empty-calendar handling.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Story definition and original acceptance criteria from epic planning. Use this for business intent and dependency sequencing.

doc.title: Database Schemas
doc.snippet: Confirms current table intent: gcal_event is the local cache, data_source_refresh is the sync bookkeeping table. The documented schema lacks sync_token, so Story 2.2 must add it explicitly.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Confirms Epic 2 service responsibilities, Google.Apis.Calendar.v3 package choice, and Polly-backed HTTP resilience requirements.

doc.title: Story 1.6
doc.snippet: Use the repo's actual logging and DI conventions: constructor-injected ILogger, services.AddLogging(builder => builder.AddSerilog()), and friendly user-visible error handling.

file.reason: Current DI and Serilog bridge pattern. New sync services must follow this registration style.
Already exposes GcalEvents, GcalEventVersions, DataSourceRefreshes, AuditLogs, and Configs. Reuse it directly.
Existing local event cache entity. Story 2.2 must map Google API results onto this schema instead of inventing a new one.
Current sync bookkeeping entity. Missing SyncToken today; Story 2.2 must add it via migration.
Needs mapping for the new sync_token column while preserving current table and index names.
Manual sync should record OperationType = gcal_sync here.
Confirms xUnit/Moq/FluentAssertions and the x64 project-reference pattern to keep tests aligned with the main project.

constraints.constraint: Use the current root-level Services/Data/ViewModels/Views layout. Do not create new Core or Repository projects for this story.
Story 2.2 must reuse Story 2.1 OAuth and token-storage services. Do not introduce a second authentication path.
Persist fetched events through the existing GcalEvent entity and CalendarDbContext.GcalEvents. Do not add a duplicate Google event table or DTO-backed persistence layer.
DataSourceRefresh currently lacks SyncToken. Story 2.2 must add a nullable sync_token column and persist Google's nextSyncToken for future incremental sync work.
Do not write version-history snapshots in this story. gcal_event_version changes belong to Story 2.3.
Use the Story 2.1 settings/auth surface for manual sync, progress, and cancel controls. Do not build a second sync UI in MainWindow.
Use constructor-injected ILogger<T> and the existing Serilog bridge. User-visible failures must be friendly and must not expose raw exception details.
Use Events.List with SingleEvents=true, ShowDeleted=true, explicit range bounds, pagination, and cancellation support.
Normalize stored timestamps to UTC before writing StartDatetime and EndDatetime. All-day events must still be represented consistently using the current GcalEvent schema.

tests.standards: xUnit + Moq + FluentAssertions. Use unit tests for pagination and mapping, and SQLite-backed integration tests for SyncManager persistence and cancellation behavior.

ideas.test: Mock three paginated Google responses and assert all items are returned before completion.
Verify all-day events map to IsAllDay = true with consistent UTC-normalized dates.
Verify recurring instances set IsRecurringInstance = true and carry RecurringEventId.
Run sync twice and assert existing rows update rather than duplicate.
Seed an existing row, sync a cancelled Google event, and assert IsDeleted = true.
Assert DataSourceRefresh stores source name, timestamps, records fetched, success, and sync token.
Cancel after the first page and assert already-written rows remain persisted while later pages are skipped.
Mock zero events and assert success with zero added rows and no error state.
